summary: Merged the local `mapData` definition in `src/melee/gr/ground.c` into the shared `Ground` struct in `src/melee/gr/types.h`, replacing hard-coded 0x204 allocations with `sizeof(Ground)`, moving known velocity/position fields into named `Vec3` members, and adding a static size assertion. This reduced duplicate layout knowledge for stage ground user data and made the `Ground` type the single source of truth for the 0x204-byte allocation.
what_changed: Changed ground user-data allocations from `HSD_MemAlloc(0x204)` to `HSD_MemAlloc(sizeof(Ground))` in multiple paths, including `alloc_user_data_ground`, `Ground_801C14D0`, and `Ground_801C1A20`.
Renamed/used callback fields through the shared struct: `x8_callback`, `xC_callback`, and `x1C_callback` instead of raw `x8`, `xC`, and `x1C`.
Collapsed individual initialization of `x20` through `x3C` to a loop over `temp_r3->x20` using `ARRAY_SIZE`.
Added `#include "platform.h"` in `ground.c`, apparently to provide `ARRAY_SIZE` and/or `STATIC_ASSERT` support used by the refactor.
Expanded `struct Ground` in `types.h` to include fields previously present in `mapData`: `Vec3 self_vel`, `Vec3 cur_pos`, `x58` through `x70`, and adjusted padding before the ground-vars union.
Changed `struct Ground`'s `x20` region from separate implied integer fields to `int x20[8]`.
Reduced the `GroundVars` union padding from `0x218 - 0xC4` to `0x204 - 0xC4`, aligning the shared `Ground` size with actual allocations.
Removed trailing per-stage ground-vars padding arrays that had padded individual `GroundVars_*` structs out to 0x218.
Added `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Ground) == 0x204)` to lock the merged layout size.
smart_moves: Made `Ground` the canonical type instead of maintaining a duplicate local `mapData` layout in `ground.c`.
Used `sizeof(Ground)` rather than the literal allocation size `0x204`, reducing future risk when the struct layout changes.
Added a static size assertion to catch accidental layout drift during future header edits.
Converted repeated sentinel assignments for contiguous fields into an `ARRAY_SIZE` loop over `x20[8]`, documenting that offsets 0x20 through 0x3C are an array-like region.
Promoted known coordinate fields into semantic `Vec3 self_vel` and `Vec3 cur_pos` members rather than keeping separate `selfVelX/Y/Z` and `posX/Y/Z` names in a local struct.
Removed excessive padding from stage-specific `GroundVars_*` structs once the enclosing `Ground` union carried the correct total-size responsibility.
decomp_lessons: When a local C file struct and a shared header struct describe the same allocation, merge them so offsets, field names, and size assertions live in one place.
Prefer `sizeof(Type)` for user-data allocation when the type is known; literal sizes like `0x204` should be guarded or eliminated.
If several adjacent unknown fields are initialized identically and accessed as a contiguous range, representing them as an array can simplify matching and clarify layout intent.
For nested variant data, keep total-object padding in the enclosing struct or union rather than padding every variant to the full object size.
Static asserts are valuable for decomp layout refactors because they preserve ABI/offset expectations while allowing field names to improve.
Replacing scalar XYZ fields with `Vec3` can improve readability without changing layout when offsets and size are preserved.
naming_conventions: Shared ground user-data type is `Ground`, replacing local `mapData`.
Velocity and position vectors are named `self_vel` and `cur_pos`.
Ground variant union is named `GroundVars` with member `gv`.
Stage-specific variant structs keep names such as `grBigBlue_GroundVars`, `grCorneria_GroundVars`, `GroundVars_flatzone`, and `grZebes_GroundVars`.
Unknown integer array at offsets 0x20-0x3C is represented as `x20[8]` rather than separate `x20`, `x24`, `x28`, etc.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved the 0x204 allocation size through `sizeof(Ground)` plus `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Ground) == 0x204)`.
Used `ARRAY_SIZE(temp_r3->x20)` for loop initialization, likely maintaining equivalent initialization while avoiding eight separate assignments.
The diff does not provide explicit reviewer discussion or before/after match status, so any effect on matching beyond layout preservation is not evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type `Ground::x20` entries beyond the array representation.
Search for subsequent uses of `self_vel` and `cur_pos` to see whether the vector naming propagated through ground/stage code.
Check whether any remaining hard-coded `HSD_MemAlloc(0x204)` ground allocations exist after this PR.
Look for later changes to `GroundVars` union size or `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Ground) == 0x204)`.
